text: I'm not good enough.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: Not good enough compared to whom? Good enough for what specifically?

text: I need to do better.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: Better than what? Better than whom? In what way specifically?

text: I can't talk to my parents about this.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: What would happen if you did? What stops you specifically?

text: I always mess everything up.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: Always? Can you think of one time when you didn't mess something up?

text: Crying is weakness.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: According to whom? Who decided that crying equals weakness?

text: My boss thinks I'm incompetent.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: How do you know what your boss thinks? Has your boss said this directly?

text: My anxiety is making me fail at work.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: How exactly does anxiety cause the failures? What is the specific mechanism?

text: Getting a bad review means I'm worthless.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: How does one review make you worthless as a person? What else would need to be true for that to follow?

text: When I get fired, my family will fall apart.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: What makes you certain you'll be fired? Is that definitely going to happen?

text: Everyone at work hates me.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: Everyone? What evidence do you have that each person hates you? Is there anyone who doesn't?

text: My relationship is a failure.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: How specifically is it failing? What would success look like?

text: I'm not managing well.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: Not managing what specifically? What would managing well look like for you?

text: Nobody ever supports me.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: Nobody? Has there been even one person in your life who has supported you?

text: I must be perfect or I'm a failure.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: What would happen if you weren't perfect? Where does this rule come from?
Is there a middle ground between perfect and failure?

text: Things will never get better.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: Never? What evidence do you have that improvement is impossible?

text: My wife knows I don't love her anymore.

patterns_detected.pattern_text: My wife knows I don't love her
patterns_detected.recovery_question: Does your wife know this, or are you assuming she does? Have you spoken about it?

text: Asking for help makes me look weak.

patterns_detected.pattern_text: asking for help makes me look weak
patterns_detected.recovery_question: What is the connection between asking for help and appearing weak? Is that the only interpretation?

text: Since my father died, I can't feel anything.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: What would happen if you allowed yourself to feel something? What gets in the way?

text: I'm a burden to everyone who loves me.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: Everyone? How do you know the people who love you experience you as a burden?

text: My presentation was a disaster.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: What specifically didn't go well? Was every part of it a disaster?

text: I should be over this by now.

patterns_detected.pattern_text: should be over this by now
patterns_detected.recovery_question: According to whom? Who decided there's a timeline for processing grief/trauma?

text: I can't set boundaries without losing people.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: What makes you certain setting boundaries means losing people? Has that always been true?

text: If I show emotion, people will think I'm unstable.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: How do you know that's what they'd think? Could there be other ways they might interpret your emotions?

text: I failed as a father because my son isn't doing well.

patterns_detected.pattern_text: son not doing well means I failed as a father
patterns_detected.recovery_question: How does your son's current struggles mean you failed? What else could explain it?

text: The pressure at work is destroying my health.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: In what specific ways is the pressure affecting your health? Are there other contributing factors?

text: I'll never be able to trust anyone again.

patterns_detected.pattern_text: never be able to trust anyone
patterns_detected.recovery_question: Never? Is there anyone in your life right now whom you trust even a little?

text: Success means I have to sacrifice my family time.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: Is that always true? Do you know successful people who also have family time?

text: I know my mother is disappointed in me.

patterns_detected.pattern_text: I know my mother is disappointed
patterns_detected.recovery_question: Has your mother told you she's disappointed, or is this an interpretation?

text: I can't be happy until I get this promotion.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: What would happiness look like right now, even without the promotion? Is happiness conditional on it?
Are you assuming the promotion is something you'll definitely get?

text: Therapy is for people who can't cope on their own.

patterns_detected.pattern_text: Therapy is for people who can't cope
patterns_detected.recovery_question: Who says that? Where does this belief come from?

text: I have no talent. I've never been good at anything.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: No talent in what area specifically?
Never? Is there truly nothing you've been good at?

text: My colleague doesn't like me; he never smiles when I walk in.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: Could there be other reasons he doesn't smile? Are you certain that's about you?

patterns_detected.pattern_text: not smiling means doesn't like me
patterns_detected.recovery_question: Is not smiling the same as disliking someone?

text: I'm only performing okay. I should be doing much better.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: According to whose standard? What does 'much better' look like?
Better than what? Better than when?

text: I've been deleted from a group chat. This means nobody wants me around.

patterns_detected.pattern_text: deleted from group chat means nobody wants me
patterns_detected.recovery_question: Is that the only explanation for being removed? What else could explain it?

text: I shouldn't complain. Others have it much worse.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: Who says you shouldn't? Does someone else's suffering invalidate your own?

text: My depression makes it impossible to function.

patterns_detected.pattern_text: depression makes it impossible to function
patterns_detected.recovery_question: In what specific areas is functioning hardest? Are there areas where you can still function?

text: I know my team thinks I'm weak for going to therapy.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: Do you know this for certain, or are you imagining their reaction?

text: I have to be strong for my family at all times.

patterns_detected.pattern_text: have to be strong at all times
patterns_detected.recovery_question: What would happen if you weren't strong for one moment? Who made this rule?
At all times? Is there any space where you're allowed to be vulnerable?

text: I failed the exam because I'm stupid.

patterns_detected.recovery_question: Is stupidity the only possible reason for failing an exam? What other factors might have contributed?

text: Everything I touch turns to disaster.

patterns_detected.pattern_text: Everything I touch turns to disaster
patterns_detected.recovery_question: Everything? Can you think of something you've touched that didn't turn into a disaster?
